dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a CHEST PAIN call at Medical Examiner's Office in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Amber from rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Sharp chest pain with upper back pain. Started about 30 minutes ago.
You're awake and fully alert-is that correct?
Yes, I'm aware of where I am.
Your eyes are open, and you don't need prompting.
Can you tell me your name and where you are?
I'm Melissa Ritter, I'm at the Medical Examiner's Office.
Can you raise both your arms for me?
I can and will do that now.
Are you having trouble breathing or any obstruction now?
No, I'm not wheezing or coughing.
Your breathing rate is 16, and no work of breathing seen. 14. Primary Assessment; check_breathing:spO2; Partner: Your oxygen saturation is 100%-very good.
Heart rate is 90-strong and regular.
Blood pressure is 131 over 82.
Skin is warm, pink, and dry.
I was at the hospital for similar chest pain before but this is worse now.
How suddenly did the pain come on-was it gradual or sudden?
It came suddenly about 30 minutes ago.
Were you doing anything when the pain began?
No, I was just sitting. 23. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T.:quality; EMT: Can you describe the chest feeling-pressure, sharp, burning?
It's a crushing pressure, like an elephant is on me.
Does it go anywhere-like back or arms?
It's in my upper midline back. 27. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T.:severity; EMT: Rate the pain from 1 to 10, how high is it?
It's a 9.
Any other symptoms besides the chest and back pain?

No, just pressure and discomfort. 31. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:medications; EMT: Currently taking any medications? 32. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:medications; Patient: None reported, but I have hypothyroidism.
Have you ever had chest pain or heart issues?
I've had chest pain before. They found elevated liver enzymes then.
When's your last time eating or drinking? 36. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:last_oral_intake; Patient: A couple hours ago, had a light breakfast.
What were you doing just before the pain started?
I was just sitting and resting in here.
Can you confirm your full name again?
My name is Melissa Ritter.
No head or neck injuries, swelling, or bruising found.
Ears, nose, and mouth appear clear with no abnormalities.
Chest looks normal. Equal rise and fall with breathing. 44. Secondary Assessment; thorax_lungs_cardio_exam:breathe_sounds; EMT: Breath sounds are clear on both sides.
Arms and legs are intact, no deformation.

Show me the back pain area again. 47. Secondary Assessment; extremities_and_back_exam:back_assessment; Patient: It's along the upper midline of my back. 48. Secondary Assessment; extremities_and_back_exam:back_assessment; EMT: Is it centralized or spreading? 49. Secondary Assessment; extremities_and_back_exam:back_assessment; Patient: It is centralized-doesn't go any other place.
Heart rate is 89 now-still strong and even.
Still breathing at 16 per minute and good depth.
Blood pressure is 111 over 64.
Oxygen is now at 99%-still good.
EKG shows normal sinus rhythm again.
First blood sugar was at 119-within normal range.
Breathing has increased now to 28.
4-lead monitoring in, still no cardiac changes.
Breathing sounds remain clear again throughout lungs.
Blood pressure is 143 over 80 now, no elevation.
Your oxygen level is 100% again.
On to 4-lead EKG for ongoing monitoring now.

4-lead is staying in place with no new issues. 63. Take Interventions; take_procedures:cv_ecg_12_lead_obtained; EMT: Performing 12-lead now. Results came back normal. 64. Take Interventions; give_medications:normal_saline; EMT: Need to start an IV for hydration now. 65. Take Interventions; give_medications:normal_saline; Partner: IV started in the left arm-using 18 gauge.
Breathing is at 19 now-slightly reduced.
Blood pressure is 137 over 86 now-within acceptable range.
SpO2 is stable again at 99%.
EKG remains normal-no ST changes or arrhythmias.
Current blood pressure is 111 over 56 with breathing of 13.
Oxygen saturation continues at 99%.
Following protocol 2-1, transporting to [COMPANY_NAME] for cardiology.
Ready to move you onto the stretcher for transport.
Harnessing you now to transfer safely.
Now secured for the ride to the ambulance.
ED nurse, 41-year-old Melissa with crushing chest and back pain, history of hypothyroidism. Vitals are 89 pulse, 13 respirations, 111/56 BP, no cardiac changes. 12-lead normal, IV in left arm.
Copy. Ready for transfer.
Completed a quick check of limbs and back-no injuries found.
Continuing check of skin and overall status-no rashes or bruising.
No head or neck trauma detected-no deformities.
Ears, nose, and throat appear clear with no signs of injury.

Glasgow is 15-eye 4, verbal 5, motor 6. 83. Secondary Assessment; head_and_neck_exam:pupils; EMT: Pupils reactive and equal-no slowness. 84. Secondary Assessment; head_and_neck_exam:mental_status; EMT: You are alert and know time, date, and location.
No findings on chest trauma or ribcage defects.
Chest remains normal with no D.C.A.P.B.T.L.S. identified.
No joint issues or deformations in upper or lower limbs.
With the pain, can you still move both arms and legs?
I can move them fine-no difficulty.
Pulse found strong and regular in both wrists. 91. Secondary Assessment; extremities_and_back_exam:skin; EMT: Skin is otherwise normal-no rashes or concern.
Last reading shows 13 for respirations, 89 pulse, and 111/56 BP.
All systems prepared for ambulance transfer. Fasten your harness.
